title: manihot carthagenensis
intro: Manihot carthagenensis, also known as the wild cassava or tapioca bush, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to treat various health issues. The leaves and roots of the plant have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
